text: Both internal and external training loads should be monitored using multidisciplinary approaches to protect athletes from injury and ill health
Internal load (physiological response) and external load (work performed) are distinct constructs that should be quantified separately for comprehensive athlete monitoring
A shared conceptual framework for training load monitoring should be used by coaches, sport science, and medical staff to standardize load tracking practices
Training load monitoring serves dual purposes: optimizing performance through appropriate loading and protecting athletes from injury and illness caused by inappropriate loading
